fs.writeFileSync(tmpPath, JSON.stringify(awInfo, null, 2));
console.log('Generated aw_info.json at:', tmpPath);

core.setOutput('model', awInfo.model);

2026-03-16T09:33:16.3695830Z The PR has been created. Here's a summary of all the inconsistencies found and fixed across the `documentation/` folder:
2026-03-16T09:33:16.3696735Z 
2026-03-16T09:33:16.3696905Z ## Changes Made
2026-03-16T09:33:16.3697129Z 
2026-03-16T09:33:16.3697436Z ### 1. `documentation/Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md`
2026-03-16T09:33:16.3702460Z **Wrong pnpm package name**: `npm install -g @pnpm` → `npm install -g pnpm`
2026-03-16T09:33:16.3703020Z 
2026-03-16T09:33:16.3707600Z The `@pnpm` scoped package doesn't exist on npm. The correct package name is `pnpm`.
2026-03-16T09:33:16.3708199Z 
2026-03-16T09:33:16.3711438Z ### 2. `documentation/resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md`
2026-03-16T09:33:17.2894750Z **Missing `/` path separator**: The combined shell one-liner's `git add` portion was missing the `/` between `` `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` `` and `pnpm-lock.yaml`, which would produce a broken path like `/home/user/azure-sdk-for-jspnpm-lock.yaml`.
2026-03-16T09:33:17.2896241Z 
2026-03-16T09:33:17.2905175Z Fixed: `` git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml `` → `` git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml ``
2026-03-16T09:33:17.2906068Z 
2026-03-16T09:33:17.2906589Z ### 3. `documentation/Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md`
2026-03-16T09:33:18.4008276Z **Outdated mocha/chai test patterns in both examples**: The codebase has fully migrated to `vitest` (1,400+ test files use it vs. 1 using mocha). Both example blocks were updated:
2026-03-16T09:33:18.4009475Z 
2026-03-16T09:33:18.4013405Z - Replaced `import { assert } from "chai"` with `import { assert, describe, it, beforeEach, afterEach } from "vitest"`
2026-03-16T09:33:24.0838065Z - Replaced `import { Context } from "mocha"` + `context.currentTest` with `VitestTestContext` from `@azure-tools/test-recorder` and `new Recorder(context)` (passing context directly)  
2026-03-16T09:33:24.0886379Z - Updated `beforeEach(async function (this: Context) {` to `beforeEach(async (ctx) => {` (vitest arrow-function style)
2026-03-16T09:33:24.0886894Z

get_status - Get status of a head commit in a pull request. This reflects status of builds and checks.\n 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.\n 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.\n 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.\n 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.\n","enum":["get","get_diff","get_status","get_files","get_review_comments","get_reviews","get_comments"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"pullNumber":{"description":"Pull request number","type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"}},"required":["method","owner","repo","pullNumber"],"type":"object"},"name":"pull_request_read"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search code"},"description":"Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. 2026-03-16T09:33:38.9804725Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-16T09:33:09.31466762Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Summary\n\nThis PR fixes three documentation inconsistencies found in the `documentation/` folder.\n\n---\n\n### 1. `documentation/Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md` — Wrong pnpm package name\n\n**Problem:** The code-gen-pipeline installation instructions referenced `npm install -g @pnpm`, but the `@pnpm` scoped package does not exist on npm. The correct package name is simply `pnpm`.\n\n**Fix:** Changed `npm install -g @pnpm` → `npm install -g pnpm`.\n\n---\n\n### 2. `documentation/resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md` — Missing `/` in `git add` command\n\n**Problem:** The combined shell command on line 16 was:\n```shell\ngit checkout upstream/main `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml \u0026\u0026 pnpm install \u0026\u0026 git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml\n```\nThe `git add` portion was missing the `/` path separator between the output of `` `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` `` and `pnpm-lock.yaml`, which would cause the command to fail (the path would be something like `/home/user/azure-sdk-for-jspnpm-lock.yaml`).\n\n**Fix:** Added the missing `/` so the `git add` command reads:\n```shell\ngit add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3. `documentation/Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` — Outdated mocha/chai examples\n\n**Problem:** Both test code examples in this document used `chai` and `mocha` imports and patterns, which are outdated. The repository has migrated to `vitest` (1,400+ test files use vitest vs. 1 using mocha). Specifically:\n\n- `import { assert } from \"chai\"` — should come from `vitest`\n- `import { Context } from \"mocha\"` — should use `VitestTestContext` from `@azure-tools/test-recorder`\n- `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` — should be `new Recorder(context)` (passing the vitest context directly)\n- `beforeEach(async function (this: Context) {` — should use vitest arrow-function style `beforeEach(async (ctx) =\u003e {`\n\n**Fix:** Updated both examples (`glossary.spec.ts` + `recordedClient.ts` for RLC, and `monitor.spec.ts` for HLC) to use current vitest patterns, matching the actual patterns used in SDK test files (e.g., `sdk/containerregistry/arm-containerregistry/test/public/`).\n","branch":"docs/fix-documentation-inconsistencies","title":"Fix documentation inconsistencies in documentation/ folder"},"name":"create_pull_request"}}}

2026-03-16T09:33:40.0701517Z ##[warning]Node.js 20 actions are deprecated. The following actions are running on Node.js 20 and may not work as expected: actions/download-artifact@018cc2cf5baa6db3ef3c5f8a56943fffe632ef53. Actions will be forced to run with Node.js 24 by default starting June 2nd, 2026. Please check if updated versions of these actions are available that support Node.js 24. To opt into Node.js 24 now, set the FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true environment variable on the runner or in your workflow file. Once Node.js 24 becomes the default, you can temporarily opt out by setting ACTIONS_ALLOW_USE_UNSECURE_NODE_VERSION=true. For more information see: https://github.blog/changelog/2025-09-19-deprecation-of-node-20-on-github-actions-runners/
